description_long: This is a traditional windmill structure featuring a tall, rectangular wooden tower set atop a stone base, with a large, pitched wooden roof covering an attached lower building. The windmill's most prominent feature is its four long, lattice-style blades, which are mounted on a central hub at the front of the tower. The stone base includes a large wooden door and small windows, suggesting interior access and functionality. Wooden ladders and steps provide access to different levels, and the attached building has a smaller roof and shuttered windows. The overall construction is primarily wood and stone, with visible fasteners and joints. The windmill sits on a flat, earth-toned base, indicating its placement in an open area such as a park or urban green space.
description: A classic wooden windmill with a stone base, four large lattice blades, and an attached lower building with a pitched roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the windmill's four blades, main entrance, and attached building with a pitched roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the attached building and the full width of the windmill's roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the windmill tower, the stone base, and the rear side of the attached building.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the ladder access to the tower and the side profile of the windmill blades.
